Case title: ResearchBench / DiscoveryBench sociology and economics discovery tasks
Short description: Use benchmark tasks drawn from sociology and economics to test whether AI supports inspiration retrieval, hypothesis composition, or ranking differently.
Empirical unit of analysis: Benchmark tasks derived from published discovery workflows
Key typology question: Should theory-building be split into inspiration retrieval and mechanism discipline rather than treated as one role?
Observable artifacts / traces: Benchmark traces, retrieved inspirations, generated hypotheses, ranking outputs
Key outcomes observed: Performance by subtask, failure concentration at composition/ranking, cross-domain stability
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: LLMs perform equally well across retrieval, composition, and ranking on social-science tasks
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Benchmarks simplify open-ended theory work and may understate institutional context
Why this case has high leverage: It is one of the few ways to decompose early-stage discovery work with controlled tasks.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
